tools.name: SEARCH
tools.description: Search for documents by semantic similarity

q.description: Up to a 400-word sentence for which you wish to find semantically similar chunks of knowledge.

n.description: Number of semantically similar chunks of text to return. Use 'n_results=3' for up to 5, and 'n_results=10' for more information. If you do not receive enough information, consider trying again with a larger 'n_results' value.

from.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format with timezone (e.g., 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z). Use this to filter results from a specific date.

to.description: End date in ISO 8601 format with timezone (e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Use this to filter results until a specific date.

tools.name: AGENTIC_SEARCH
tools.description: Search for documents by semantic similarity

query.description: Up to a 400-word sentence for which you wish to find semantically similar chunks of knowledge.

user_context.description: The additional context for the query. You might need to summarize the conversation up to this point for better context-awared results.

n_chunks.description: Number of semantically similar chunks of text to return. Use 'n_results=3' for up to 5, and 'n_results=10' for more information. If you do not receive enough information, consider trying again with a larger 'n_results' value.

from.description: Start date in ISO 8601 format with timezone (e.g., 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z). Use this to filter results from a specific date.

to.description: End date in ISO 8601 format with timezone (e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Use this to filter results until a specific date.

tools.name: LIST_DOCUMENTS
tools.description: Retrieves a paginated list of all documents in the system.

tools.name: MEMORIZE
tools.description: Save a piece of text information in your Rememberizer account to help you discover semantically similar knowledge in the future.

name.description: Name of the information. This is used to identify the information in the future.

content.description: The information you wish to memorize.
